message:
feat(compaction+session): integrate background compaction worker into AgentSession and add JSONL→V2 migration with atomic verification

Background compaction worker integration (agent.rs, compaction_worker.rs, lib.rs):
- Add CompactionWorkerState to AgentSession, wiring the new compaction_worker
  module into the agent lifecycle so LLM-driven compaction runs off the
  foreground turn path on a dedicated thread
- Refactor maybe_compact() into a two-phase non-blocking design:
  Phase 1 applies any completed background compaction result via try_recv();
  Phase 2 checks quota gates (cooldown, max attempts, pending state) and
  spawns a new background thread if allowed
- Separate compact_now() (synchronous, used by /compact slash command) from
  the async two-phase path to preserve existing explicit-compact behavior
- CompactionQuota provides configurable cooldown (60s default), timeout (120s),
  and max_attempts_per_session (100) to bound resource usage

JSONL→V2 migration tooling (session.rs):
- Add migrate_jsonl_to_v2() which atomically creates a V2 sidecar from a JSONL
  session, verifies fidelity (entry count, hash chain, index consistency), and
  records a MigrationEvent in the V2 store's migration ledger
- Add verify_v2_against_jsonl() for post-migration fidelity checking that
  compares entry IDs in order and validates internal V2 checksums
- Fix create_v2_sidecar_from_jsonl() to remove pre-existing sidecar directory
  before recreation, preventing stale segment accumulation on re-migration

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
